title: Nursery Rhyme. I. Historical - Old King Cole
content:
Old King Cole
        Was a merry old soul,
        And a merry old soul was he;
        He called for his pipe,
        And he called for his bowl,
        And he called for his fiddlers three.
        Every fiddler, he had a fiddle,
        And a very fine fiddle had he;
        Twee tweedle dee, tweedle dee, went the fiddlers.
        Oh, there's none so rare,
        As can compare
        With King Cole and his fiddlers three!
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. I. Historical - Old King Cole"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
